items.prompt: Count the collection accurately and state how you know the final count represents the whole set.
items.expected_evidence: choose the numeral that names a shown quantity
items.scoring_guidance: Meets when the learner demonstrates choose the numeral that names a shown quantity with mathematically valid structure.

items.prompt: Compose and decompose the number 14 in two different ways.
items.expected_evidence: build or select a quantity that matches a shown numeral
items.scoring_guidance: Meets when the learner demonstrates build or select a quantity that matches a shown numeral with mathematically valid structure.

items.prompt: Compare 348 and 384 and justify the comparison by place value.
items.expected_evidence: reject mismatched numeral labels on bounded sets
items.scoring_guidance: Meets when the learner demonstrates reject mismatched numeral labels on bounded sets with mathematically valid structure.

items.prompt: Explain why the last counting word tells how many objects are in the set.
items.expected_evidence: choose the numeral that names a shown quantity
items.scoring_guidance: Meets when the learner explains the structure coherently and ties the explanation back to choose the numeral that names a shown quantity.

items.prompt: Explain how the value of a digit depends on its place.
items.expected_evidence: build or select a quantity that matches a shown numeral
items.scoring_guidance: Meets when the learner explains the structure coherently and ties the explanation back to build or select a quantity that matches a shown numeral.

items.prompt: A student counts objects twice and reports the larger total. Diagnose the one-to-one correspondence error. Focus on count symbol disconnect.
items.expected_evidence: choose the numeral that names a shown quantity
items.scoring_guidance: Meets when the learner identifies the dominant breakdown, names the structural issue accurately, and redirects the work toward choose the numeral that names a shown quantity.

items.prompt: A student says 402 is greater than 39 because 2 is greater than 9. Identify the place-value mistake. Focus on symbol quantity guessing.
items.expected_evidence: build or select a quantity that matches a shown numeral
items.scoring_guidance: Meets when the learner identifies the dominant breakdown, names the structural issue accurately, and redirects the work toward build or select a quantity that matches a shown numeral.

items.prompt: Use the same number idea with objects, numerals, and spoken language in a less-familiar representation.
items.expected_evidence: choose the numeral that names a shown quantity
items.scoring_guidance: Meets when the learner preserves choose the numeral that names a shown quantity in a changed surface form or representation.

items.prompt: Show the same quantity with a drawing, a numeral, and a decomposition statement.
items.expected_evidence: build or select a quantity that matches a shown numeral
items.scoring_guidance: Meets when the learner preserves build or select a quantity that matches a shown numeral in a changed surface form or representation.

items.prompt: After a delay, compare or compose a new whole number and explain the reasoning used.
items.expected_evidence: choose the numeral that names a shown quantity
items.scoring_guidance: Meets when the learner still demonstrates choose the numeral that names a shown quantity on a delayed recheck without a blocking recurrence.

items.prompt: Probe compare small quantities before closing quantity symbol correspondence, and state whether the learner is blocked by an upstream knowledge gap.
items.expected_evidence: shows whether compare small quantities is secure enough to support quantity symbol correspondence
items.scoring_guidance: Meets when the learner response makes it possible to distinguish an upstream gap in compare small quantities from a current-node issue in quantity symbol correspondence.

items.prompt: Probe numeral recognition and writing readiness before closing quantity symbol correspondence, and state whether the learner is blocked by an upstream knowledge gap.
items.expected_evidence: shows whether numeral recognition and writing readiness is secure enough to support quantity symbol correspondence
items.scoring_guidance: Meets when the learner response makes it possible to distinguish an upstream gap in numeral recognition and writing readiness from a current-node issue in quantity symbol correspondence.
